original_query: SELECT "EMP0"."ENAME" FROM (SELECT * FROM "EMP" WHERE "ENAME" = 'A') AS "t0" LEFT JOIN "EMP" AS "EMP0" ON "t0"."JOB" = "EMP0"."JOB" GROUP BY "EMP0"."ENAME"

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replace SELECT * in the subquery with SELECT JOB since only the JOB column is needed for the join, reducing the amount of data processed
Replace GROUP BY with DISTINCT which can potentially eliminate the sort operation while maintaining the same result set
